message:
feat(wizard): add prominent connection drop reassurance AlertCard

Add a visible "What if my connection drops?" AlertCard near the install
command (not buried in SimplerGuide) that:
- Reassures users with "Don't panic!"
- Explains the installer keeps running on VPS
- Shows the 3-step recovery process
- Emphasizes the installer is idempotent (safe to re-run)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -535,6 +535,31 @@ export default function LaunchOnboardingPage() { + +

+ The problem: When you SSH into your VPS and then close your laptop + or lose internet, your terminal session dies. Any running commands stop. +

+

+ The solution: tmux creates "sessions" that keep + running on the VPS even when you disconnect. Think of it like leaving a TV playing in another room + — it keeps going whether you're watching or not. +

+

+ NTM (Named Tmux Manager) makes tmux easier. Instead of cryptic commands, + you get simple ones: +

+
    +
  • ntm new myproject — Start a new session
    • +
  • ntm attach myproject — Resume a session
    • +
  • ntm list — See all your sessions
    • +
+

+ This is why you can start a Claude task, close your laptop, go to bed, and come back + to find it completed. The session keeps running on the VPS. +

+ +

@@ -10,6 +10,7 @@ import { Rocket, ShieldCheck, Code, + Wifi, } from "lucide-react"; import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button"; import { CommandCard } from "@/components/command-card"; @@ -118,6 +119,24 @@ export default function RunInstallerPage() { /> + {/* Connection drop reassurance */} + +
+

+ Don't panic! If your SSH connection drops during installation: +

+
    +
  1. The installer keeps running on the VPS
    2. +
  2. Just SSH back in using the same command
    3. +
  3. Run the installer command again — it will resume where it left off
    4. +
+

+ The installer is designed to be run multiple times safely. If anything fails, + you can always re-run it. +

+
+ + {/* Transparency & trust */}
